title: cuphea micropetala var. hirtella
intro: Cuphea micropetala var. hirtella is a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae, commonly known as firecracker plant or red cuphea. This plant is a shrub-like perennial native to Mexico, with vibrant red or yellow flowers and a hairy, red-tinged stem. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cuphea micropetala var. hirtella include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support cardiovascular health, and it can be prepared as a tea or infused in oil for herbal remedies.
